title: 'Hereditary' Is the Most Disturbing Horror Movie in Years, With Ari Aster
format: Sean Fennessey talks to writer-director Ari Aster about 'Hereditary,' the horror films that scarred him as a kid, and the last great things he's seen.

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Aster's debut, pitched to his crew not as horror but as 'a family tragedy that curdles into a nightmare.' The original cut ran three hours; roughly 30 scenes were cut for pace. Toni Collette plays a miniaturist matriarch, so the house interiors were built on a stage and duplicated in miniature.

films.title: Carrie
films.note: The De Palma film that troubled Aster at 11: its images kept him up for years, afraid to walk around in the dark. On a recent rewatch he found it campy and 'deeply, deeply sad.'

films.title: Dick Tracy
films.note: The first movie his mom took him to see; a Tommy-gun scene made him bolt out of his seat and run down five city blocks. He now reads it as high-level camp: a devoted, built-world comic-book aesthetic.

films.title: Thor: Ragnarok
films.note: Offered as a modern comic-book movie that's fun and bright, but achieved in CGI rather than the tactile world Dick Tracy built.

films.title: The Cook, the Thief, His Wife & Her Lover
films.note: Peter Greenaway. Traumatized Aster as a kid: 'It feels evil to me.'

films.title: Dogville
films.note: Another film whose images seared into him: the same low hum of dread he wanted in Hereditary.

films.title: In the Bedroom
films.note: Screened for the Hereditary crew. Its turn about 30 minutes in, which changes the family and the movie, is what Aster was after; he likens the mechanism to Psycho.

films.title: Psycho
films.note: The structural comparison for In the Bedroom's mid-film swerve; Aster notes Hereditary has 'our shower scene.'

films.title: The Sixth Sense
films.note: People liken Hereditary to it via Collette; Aster sees them as superficially similar opposites: the Shyamalan film is hopeful and nice by the end, while Hereditary aims to upset.

films.title: Munchausen
films.note: Aster's short, where he first did some of the practical effects work that carried into Hereditary.

films.title: 45 Years
films.note: Andrew Haigh. Aster calls it 'an all-timer' and 'a perfect film,' has seen it many times, and screened it for the Hereditary crew.

films.title: Lean on Pete
films.note: Named as another Andrew Haigh film (Fennessey notes Haigh had recently been on the show for it).

films.title: Isle of Dogs
films.note: Wes Anderson. Aster falls on and off the Anderson wagon but found this 'staggering as an aesthetic achievement.'

films.title: You Were Never Really Here
films.note: Lynne Ramsay. Aster loves how pulpy it is and how it gets at PTSD; praises the editing, Joaquin Phoenix, and Jonny Greenwood's score.

The Cook, the Thief, His Wife & Her Love.title: The Cook, the Thief, His Wife & Her Lover
